description: Visual presentation of restaurant menu items with prices in SAR, featuring dish names in both Arabic and English, item costs, and personal recommendations or ratings to drive menu discovery and ordering.
structural_recipe: Post 1-3 menu items with: (1) Dish name in Arabic + English transliteration, (2) Price in SAR with ر.س symbol, (3) Brief descriptor or rating (⭐️, لذيذ, انصح فية), (4) Tag restaurant handle and location, (5) Use bullet points or dashes for scanability, (6) Include dish photo showcasing presentation. Optional: mention special occasion menu (Ramadan) or cuisine type (French, Turkish steak).
why_it_works: Saudi diners rely on peer recommendations and transparent pricing before dining. Showing specific menu items with clear costs in local currency builds trust, reduces decision friction, and leverages social proof through personal endorsements. Bilingual presentation respects language preferences across demographics.
transplantation_caveats: Price sensitivity: ensure SAR pricing is competitive and transparent to avoid perception of overpricing
Halal certification not mentioned in examples but critical for Saudi F&B—consider adding if applicable
Ramadan-specific menus require seasonal timing; off-season use may reduce relevance
English transliteration quality varies; poor spelling undermines credibility
cultural_constraints: Alcohol not visible or mentioned (both examples feature non-alcoholic F&B)
Halal sourcing implicit but may benefit from explicit certification
Pricing transparency aligns with Saudi consumer expectations
Bilingual captions reflect Saudi audience's mixed language literacy
